corrections.description: Article is cut off mid-sentence. The text continues beyond 'I think this is the secret of' with additional content about the relationship between Indian children and foster parents.
corrections.text: a good relationship between the Indian child and the foster parents—talk things over, tell them whatever it is that is bothering you. The foster parents should also tell the Indian child how they feel. I am thankful for the wise advice I received from the caseworkers I have had.

corrections.description: Article file contains completely wrong content - it has a story about Joan and Joey instead of the poem 'Perennials' by Marian Crawford Anderson
corrections.text:
Perennials
Marian Crawford Anderson

I have three flowers in my garden
I tend with loving care,
For they will grow and days will come
When they will not be there.

Three little girls all in bloom—
A bright bouquet to light a room.

Oh, I am seldom lonely anymore;
They bring the wide world to my door,
And take me with them, hand in hand,
Into a more enchanted land.

corrections.description: Article file starts with content from middle/end of lesson instead of the beginning shown in page image
corrections.text:
Elder Roy W. Doxey

Lesson 68—Knowledge and Events of the Future

(Text: Doctrine and Covenants, Section 88:76-116)

For First Meeting, January 1966

Objective: To learn the importance of missionary preparation; the place of education in the gospel; and of events of the future predicted.

INTRODUCTION

Among the many great truths revealed in Section 88, known as the "Olive Leaf," is the promise of seeing the Savior in his own time and in his own way, provided that the Church member will keep his mind single to God that light may dissipate darkness from mind and body (D&C 88: 67-68).
